svg.title: Hamster mini-peek v1 (scripted)
svg.desc: Mini-mode peek state for clawd-on-desk / MiniCPM-Desk-Pet, right-edge variant (host mirrors for left edge). Triggered when the cursor is over the pet (mouseOverPet): the host pushes the window ~25px into the screen (200ms easeOutQuad) and the hamster reacts curiously — head-tilt about the neck (187,252) via the peek-head-tilt wrapper, raised brows (表情板 surprised path), a small happy open-mouth (attention/happy door-rig, doorInset tuned smaller so the mouth corners stay rounded at low opening), tuft perk from the gravity rest (-32° -> -14°), and a damped sunglasses inertia bob — while both paws stay clamped to the screen edge and slide along it (peek-hands-ext, screen x+ = master y+, paw transforms untouched) with a palm motion-trail (#d8a686, appears during the slide, fades after settle), and the tie blade swings from the push inertia about the knot. NO head turn — the cursor sits on the face so gaze-tracking is meaningless (this is the difference from mini-idle). Default host mode holds the post-peek pose because the real window push/pull is handled by the app; add ?loop=1 for the self-contained showcase/demo rest→push→hold→pull cycle. svg root keeps pointer-events:none. Exposes window.__miniPeek. Pure paths + soft-lift filter, no image/gradient/fetch.
